items.note: A prevention procedure exists (puppy housing movement); there is no treatment protocol for infected animals.
No document covers panleukopenia identification, isolation, or treatment.
The most common shelter illness; no treatment or monitoring protocol.
No identification, lime-sulfur treatment, or clearance protocol.
Rabies quarantines are covered; there is no general outbreak response plan (closure criteria, testing, communication).
Quarantine documents cover rabies observation only, not routine isolation for infectious disease.
No protocol for bottle feeding, warmth, or fading kitten emergencies.

items.topic: Population management and capacity for care
items.note: No document ties intake, housing capacity, and daily population decisions together.
No SOP for structured daily walk-throughs checking every animal's status and pathway.
In progress per the consolidation; will define the TNR, CCP, and RTF outcome types.
No plan for intaking dozens of animals at once from a single case.
Kennel and area cleaning SOPs exist; no facility-wide sanitation program document.
No standalone evacuation SOP; the Facility ERP covers portions.
No SOP for animals missing from inventory.
No document covers training, certification, and competency requirements.
Existing call center documents predate current triage practice.
Policy 000-12 requires a Time Clock SOP that was never written.
